title: sorbus crenata
intro: Sorbus crenata, also known as the Manchurian mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its white flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Sorbus crenata include reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, as well as its potential in treating various gastrointestinal problems. Herbal preparations of Sorbus crenata typically involve the use of its bark, leaves, and roots, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
